Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Prime Cars Leasing Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Prime Cars Leasing Pte Ltd was incorporated
Supreme Pte Ltd leased 23 cars from PCL
Supreme Pte Ltd stopped paying monthly rentals to PCL
Ms Lim directed the sale of 14 cars to Zenith

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for selling company assets at an undervalue and retaining the sale proceeds
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interests of the company, exercise reasonable diligence, and not misappropriate corporate assets or enter into unlawful conspiracies
Party_Details.Application: The sole director breached her fiduciary duties and engaged in an unlawful conspiracy by selling the company's cars at undervalued prices without authority and transferring the proceeds to her personal bank account.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the claims for breach of fiduciary duties and conspiracy, finding that the director acted in the company's best interests to navigate a liquidity crisis. However, the court allowed the plaintiff's partial claim for the return of the remaining sale proceeds held on trust by the director.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Lim Siew Ling
Counsel: Suja Susan Thomas d/o B Thomas

Facts.Text: Ms Lim was the sole director and shareholder of PCL on record at the material time
Ms Lim repossessed 13 cars from Supreme Pte Ltd and sold 14 cars to Zenith
Ms Lim requested Zenith to transfer the balance sale proceeds to her personal bank account to pay off PCL's urgent operating expenses

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director breached fiduciary duties by selling company assets and transferring the proceeds to her personal account during a financial crisis
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act honestly and use reasonable diligence in the discharge of duties, taking actions that are commercially justifiable in the best interests of the company
Party_Details.Application: The sale of the cars and the retention of the sale proceeds were commercially justifiable actions taken in the company's best interests to ensure its survival amidst a deliberately manufactured liquidity crisis.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the director acted honestly and with reasonable diligence in the best interests of the company during a liquidity crisis, and therefore did not breach her fiduciary duties. However, she was ordered to return the remaining balance of the sale proceeds which she held on trust for the company.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Zenith Automobile Pte Ltd
Counsel: Suja Susan Thomas d/o B Thomas

Facts.Text: Zenith Automobile Pte Ltd is a Singapore-incorporated company whose business is to buy and sell used cars
During the first sale transaction, Zenith's sole director, Mr Tan, conducted an ACRA search and found that Ms Lim was PCL's sole director and shareholder, and that there were no charges against PCL

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can be held legally responsible for purchasing assets at undervalued prices and assisting a director's breach of fiduciary duties
Party_Details.Rule: A purchaser must not knowingly participate in a transaction that constitutes a contravention of a director's fiduciary duty or engage in an unlawful means conspiracy
Party_Details.Application: The purchase of the cars was transacted at arm's length at fair market value and conducted in good faith, completely negating any allegations of dishonest assistance or knowing receipt.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of undervaluation, dishonest assistance, or conspiracy by Zenith, noting that the sales were conducted at arm's length.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Lim Siew Ling
Counsel: Suja Susan Thomas d/o B Thomas

Facts.Text: Ms Lim was the sole director and shareholder of PCL
Supreme Pte Ltd stopped paying monthly rentals to PCL
Ms Lim transferred $289,700.47 to her personal account for PCL expenses.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can counterclaim for alleged outstanding director's salary and transport allowance
Party_Details.Rule: A claim for remuneration must be supported by credible evidence and valid corporate resolutions
Party_Details.Application: The director is entitled to be reimbursed and counterclaims for the outstanding director's salary and transport allowance owed to her by the company.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the counterclaim, finding that the director's evidence regarding her salary and transport allowance was inconsistent and contradictory, and the corporate resolution she relied upon was highly suspicious.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Prime Cars Leasing Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Prime Cars Leasing Pte Ltd was incorporated
PCL's monthly rental income from Supreme Pte Ltd ceased in May 2018
PCL sold 14 cars to Zenith between 2018-07-26 and 2018-08-23

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible to pay a director's alleged outstanding salary and transport allowance
Party_Details.Rule: A company is not liable to pay remuneration that is not properly authorised or supported by credible evidence
Party_Details.Application: The company is not liable for the alleged outstanding director's salary and transport allowance as the claims are unsubstantiated and contradict the established remuneration arrangements within the corporate group.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the director's claims for salary and transport allowance were not credible and dismissed the counterclaim.
